title: lathyrus palustris var. pubescens
intro: Lathyrus palustris var. pubescens, commonly known as the hairy marsh pea or hairy vetchling, is a variety of flowering plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting respiratory health. Lathyrus palustris var. pubescens can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its safety and effectiveness may vary depending on the specific preparation and individual tolerance.
